retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

Previous

©

Next

What do you need to study, how does the test day work, and what do you need to keep in mind? We’ve put together all the information you need – so you can successfully get through the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

[Through the entrance exam](/en/through-the-entrance-exam)

Previous

©

retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. Starting from the beginning of the admission period, you will be automatically admitted to the additional degree programme within five working days. Check the status of your admission in u:space > Study overview. If you have not been admitted within this timeframe, please contact the Admission Office via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933).

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: S
retrieved.text: lecturer nor the SPL can solve. - If you have questions about admission and the tuition fee, please contact the Admission Office. - In individual cases, the ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende') of the Federal Ministry can help you to find a solution together with the bodies and members of the educational institution. Related Links: - [My rights and obligations](/en/study-organisation/#c253591) - [Directorate of studies ('Studienprogrammleitung')](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Accessible Studying Team ('Team Barrierefrei') - [Office of Studienpräses (in German)](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Admission office](https://slw.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/admission-office/) - [Ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende')](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/) - [Curriculum – the legal basis for degree programmes (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) - [Statutes of the University of Vienna – study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) - [University law (2002 Universities Act, Part II: Study Law) (in German)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128) - Study plan former term for curriculum Related entries: - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27) - Studying at several universities You can study at several Austrian universities at the same time. It is also possible to study at a university and a university of applied sciences or a university college of teacher education at the same time. However, you are not allowed to study the same degree programme at two different universities. Example: Maria studies on the bachelor's programme in History at the University of Salzburg. Therefore, she cannot be admitted to the bachelor's programme History at the University of Vienna. However, she can be admitted, for example, to the bachelor's

retrieved.title: Additional master programme
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the [curriculum](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/) of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Change of master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the [curriculum](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/) of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

reranked.title: Change of master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

selected.title: Deadlines and dates
selected.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

Previous

©

Next

What do you need to study, how does the test day work, and what do you need to keep in mind? We’ve put together all the information you need – so you can successfully get through the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

[Through the entrance exam](/en/through-the-entrance-exam)

Previous

©

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:

Question: On which dates does the SPL English and American Studies open course registration for summer semester 2026?

generate.raw_response: The SPL English and American Studies opens course registration for summer semester 2026 on 06.02.2026 at 12:00 and ends on 20.02.2026 at 12:00 [1].

answer: The SPL English and American Studies opens course registration for summer semester 2026 on 06.02.2026 at 12:00 and ends on 20.02.2026 at 12:00 [1].
